observations.recommendation: The Supply Unit/Office and the Accounting Office conduct reconciliation of their records to ascertain the discrepancy and to make the necessary adjustments to come up with a reconciled “Land” account balance;

The Supply Unit prioritize the immediate registration and titling of unregistered/untitled lots acquired through purchase or donation in the name of the Municipality to properly safeguard its interest and so that ownership thereof would be absolute and established for all legal intents and purposes;
The Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for each accountable officer/collection clerks of the municipality.
Immediately enforce from the concerned accountable officers the proper accounting and liquidation of the noted unliquidated cash advances; and

Henceforth, ensure that all cash advances granted to any accountable officer is liquidated within the reglementary period and that no additional cash advance is granted to those AO with pending/unliquidated cash advances to promote timely recording in the books of accounts.

Close the DBP account of MEEDO as authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) through an SB Resolution by transferring the remaining balance thereof in the LBP MEEDO account so that only one account for such fund shall be maintained pursuant to Section 3 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

The Municipal Treasurer trace up all purchases of accountable forms and corresponding issuances thereof per Invoice and Receipts and Report of Collections and Deposits records from prior years to present as basis for preparation of CRAAF. If warranted, a personnel be assigned to specifically do the same in order to establish ending balances of accountable forms and reconcile any differences with the actual balances and consequently set in place control over the receipts and issuance of accountable forms;
Henceforth, ensure that individual RAAF from each collecting/ accountable officers are prepared and submitted; and
The Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the monthly CRAAF, duly supported with the individual RAAFs, to the Office of the Auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.

The Appraisal and Inventory Committee determine the appraised value of donated properties and those unserviceable proper-ties found on station during the inventory taking so that these properties can be correctly recorded/ adjusted in the books of accounts of the municipality; and
The General Services Office (GSO) and Accounting Office conduct reconciliation of their PPE records (PCs and PPELCs) to ascertain the discrepancy and to make the necessary adjustments, if any.
The MA, in coordination with the Supply Officer, trace back all inventories already issued based on the RIS and stock card maintained by the GSO; and
The MA make the appropriate adjusting entries based on the result of reconciliation to properly account those already issued inventories in the books of the Municipality.

Require the contractor to pay the unaccomplished work and impose the necessary liquidated damages from the delay/non completion of the project in accordance with the program of works without prejudice to other courses of action and remedies available under the circumstances. Likewise, please take note of other deficiencies noted, and make the necessary action to address or prevent further maintenance cost by the municipality.
Direct the MA to prepare and submit the supporting schedules of the reported balances of the agency’s Receivable Account; and

Henceforth, ensure that the reported receivable account of the agency is existent and valid by making sure that the required SLs of the controlling accounts of the agency are timely prepared and/or updated coupled with complete supporting documents.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Treasurer hasten the adaptation of the ETRACS in order to timely provide to the Municipal Accountant the  certified list of taxpayers with their corresponding amount due and collectible for the year as basis by the latter in setting up RPT and SET Receivables;
Meanwhile, the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the actual RPT and SET Receivables using the amounts per Realty Tax Delinquency Listing as of December 31, 2024 prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer; and
The Municipal Accountant set-up RPT and SET Receivables and corresponding Deferred RPT and SET Income at the beginning of the year based on the certified list from the Municipal Treasurer or the Municipal Assessor’s Report on Real Property Assessment in its absence.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile the noted difference of ₱121,403.46 by comparing their respective records in order to identify the cause of such difference and effect the necessary and applicable adjustments/corrections to come up with the accurate Cash-Local Treasury account balance; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant conduct periodic and timely reconciliation between their records so that errors or any other reconciling items will be immediately corrected/updated.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Expedite the implementation of unutilized balances of the related PPAs under continuing appropriation, while the unutilized balances of those PPAs no longer feasible for implementation or PPAs already completed shall be made available for other DRRM activities; and
Ensure that programs funded from unspent LDRRMF in the STF are implemented as planned.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit intact to BIR, GSIS, Pag-Ibig and PhilHealth all the contributions and/or loan amortizations withheld and within the respective prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges;
The Municipal Accountant make verification, analysis, and validation of the noted over-remittances or negative difference in the Due to Pag-Ibig account. Should it be established that there is indeed over remittance, make representation with the concerned agency for its refund, or enforce payment from the concerned/liable personnel if the same is caused by non-withholding; and
Trace up the details of the noted long outstanding Due to GSIS, Pag-Ibig and Philhealth balances under SEF and TF and make the necessary adjustments, as appropriate.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The MPDC and Municipal Engineer continually ensure that only PPAs which are ready for implementation are included in agency’s annual investment program and that procurement timelines are consistently followed to maintain high utilization and implementation rate of the 20% DF;
Reevaluate the viability of the appropriation for the purchase of lot, and construction thereon of Multi-Purpose Building/Bagsakan Center totaling ₱2 million, if found not yet timely possible, consider realigning the same for other priority developmental projects which are procurement-and-implementation-ready; and
Ensure the reversion to the general fund of the savings or unutilized balance from already completed projects amounting to ₱73,845.91.
The Municipal Planning and Development Council ensure that improvements and public infrastructures are constructed on the lot absolutely owned by the Municipality.
The Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements from TF are supported with CAF as basis for fund availability and appropriateness of the charges therefrom.
The MDRRMC ensure to program the use of LDRRMF under STF and that same is incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.

Refrain from paying regular and recurring expenses thru reimbursement or petty cash fund, instead make use of the Bids and Awards Committee for bulk acquisition for transparent procurement process in accordance with RA No. 9184; and
Limit the use of PCF for non-recurring, emergency and petty expenses only of the LGU.

Ensure the immediate implementation of the additional scope of work equivalent to ₱348,740.15 and notify the Audit Team upon its completion together with proof such as pictures before, during and after its construction; and

The Municipal Engineer ensure that a thorough inspection of project accomplishments reported by contractor is made before recommending acceptance and/or payment thereof so as to secure the interest of the government.

Open a separate depository account for the 20% DF through a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution and transfer thereto the unspent fund balance of the 20% DF and thereafter, ensure the regular transfer thereto of the 20% share from Internal Revenue Allotment (now National Tax Allotment) from the GF bank account; and
The Municipal Accountant maintain a separate books of account for the 20% DF complete with subsidiary ledgers to keep track of the transactions and balances under the fund.

Retain the DBP account of GF in consideration with the existing loans of the municipal employees in the DBP, wherein deposits and disbursements/withdrawals to be made shall be limited only to the monthly salary deductions for loan amortizations payable to DBP;
The LBP account for GF shall be the only depository account of all collection receipts under such fund and shall be used for all GF disbursements for proper control and monitoring;
The BAC secure copies of Mayor’s/Business Permit and PhilGEPS Registration Number in its procurement thru small value procurement prior recommendation to HOPE to award the contract; and

The Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements are properly supported with complete documentation by requiring the submission of Mayor’s/Business Permit and PhilGEPS Registration Number of participating suppliers in case the municipality resorted to Small Value Procurement as its mode of procurement.

We recommended and the Management agreed to create a GAD database containing gender statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data which shall be regularly updated as basis in GAD Planning, programming and policy making.

We recommended and the Management agreed to modify/reconstitute its existing GFPS to conform with the structure and composition required under Section 4.1(2) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01.

Immediately remit the remaining withheld taxes of prior months and make representation with the Bureau of Internal Revenue for the refund of the over-remittance of ₱12,219.23 under GF-MEEDO, if warranted; and

The Municipal Accounting Office expedite the tracing up and reconciliation of the over remittances.  Should it be established that there is indeed over remittance, payment from the concerned/liable personnel should be enforced;

The MT and MSWDO provide the complete set of documents indicated in the “Remarks’ column of Appendix D such as photocopy of ID, and specimen signatures as supporting documents to certain beneficiaries in the payroll;

The Appraisal and Inventory Committee determine the appraised value of donated properties and those unserviceable proper-ties found on station during the inventory taking so that these properties can be correctly recorded/ adjusted in the books of accounts of the municipality; and

The General Services Office (GSO) and Accounting Office conduct reconciliation of their PPE records (PCs and PPELCs) to ascertain the discrepancy and to make the necessary adjustments, if any.

The MA, in coordination with the Supply Officer, trace back all inventories already issued based on the RIS and stock card maintained by the GSO; and

The MA make the appropriate adjusting entries based on the result of reconciliation to properly account those already issued inventories in the books of the Municipality.

The MT and MSWDO provide the complete set of documents indicated in the “Remarks’ column of Appendix D such as photocopy of ID, and specimen signatures as supporting documents to certain beneficiaries in the payroll;

Require the contractor to pay the unaccomplished work and impose the necessary liquidated damages from the delay/non completion of the project in accordance with the program of works without prejudice to other courses of action and remedies available under the circumstances. Likewise, please take note of other deficiencies noted, and make the necessary action to address or prevent further maintenance cost by the municipality.

Direct the MA to prepare and submit the supporting schedules of the reported balances of the agency’s Receivable Account; and

Henceforth, ensure that the reported receivable account of the agency is existent and valid by making sure that the required SLs of the controlling accounts of the agency are timely prepared and/or updated coupled with complete supporting documents.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Accounting Office, in coordination with the Supply Unit/Office, exert unified effort in establishing the details of those unreconciled PPEs and same be included in its PPELCs maintained in order to ensure that balances of PPEs appearing on the financial statements are supported and reconciled with the balances reflected in the PPELCs;
The Appraisal and Inventory Committee conduct physical count of all PPEs  including PPEs like Land Improvements, Infrastructure Assets, and all Buildings and Other Structures owned by the municipality, and ensure that RPCPPE is prepared and signed in accordance with the requirement prescribed in Volume II of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and
Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Supply Unit/Office and the Accounting Office conduct reconciliation of their records to ascertain the discrepancy and to make the necessary adjustments to come up with a reconciled “Land” account balance;
The Supply Unit prioritize the immediate registration and titling of unregistered/untitled lots acquired through purchase or donation in the name of the Municipality to properly safeguard its interest and so that ownership thereof would be absolute and established for all legal intents and purposes; and
The Municipal Planning and Development Council ensure that improvements and public infrastructures are constructed on the lot absolutely owned by the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accountant expedite the reconciliation of unidentified balances in the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs aggregating ₱14.950 million and ₱3.721 million, respectively, and make the necessary adjustments. Likewise, trace/review the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) and/or coordinate with the SA to determine the proper disposition of each fund transfers. Otherwise, revert the unutilized balances to the SA in accordance with existing rules and regulations;
The Municipal Accountant review the MOAs and/or coordinate with the SA regarding reversion of the unutilized balances from completed projects presented in Table 6 and check the timeline of project implementation of the ₱100,000.00 grant for the development of Model Adolescent Friendly Health Facility based on the MOA if still implementable, otherwise, revert the same to the SA; and
The Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements from TF are supported with CAF as basis for fund availability and appropriateness of the charges therefrom.
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for each accountable officer/collection clerks of the municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Trace up and reconcile the details of the unliquidated advances and effect the necessary correcting/adjusting entries;
Immediately enforce from the concerned accountable officers the proper accounting and liquidation of the noted unliquidated cash advances; and
Henceforth, ensure that all cash advances granted to any accountable officer is liquidated within the reglementary period and that no additional cash advance is granted to those AO with pending/unliquidated cash advances to promote timely recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Expedite the implementation of unutilized balances of the related PPAs under the continuing appropriation, while the unutilized balances of those PPAs no longer feasible for implementation or PPAs already completed shall be made available for other DRRM activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP; and
The MDRRMC ensure to program the use of LDRRMF under STF and that same is incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Refrain from paying regular and recurring expenses thru reimbursement or petty cash fund, instead make use of the Bids and Awards Committee for bulk acquisition for transparent procurement process in accordance with RA No. 9184; and
Limit the use of PCF for non-recurring, emergency and petty expenses only of the LGU.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Ensure the immediate implementation of the additional scope of work equivalent to ₱348,740.15 and notify the Audit Team upon its completion together with proof such as pictures before, during and after its construction; and
The Municipal Engineer ensure that a thorough inspection of project accomplishments reported by contractor is made before recommending acceptance and/or payment thereof so as to secure the interest of the government.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Open a separate depository account for the 20% DF through a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution and transfer thereto the unspent fund balance of the 20% DF and thereafter, ensure the regular transfer thereto of the 20% share from Internal Revenue Allotment (now National Tax Allotment) from the GF bank account; and
The Municipal Accountant maintain a separate books of account for the 20% DF complete with subsidiary ledgers to keep track of the transactions and balances under the fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Retain the DBP account of GF in consideration with the existing loans of the municipal employees in the DBP, wherein deposits and disbursements/withdrawals to be made shall be limited only to the monthly salary deductions for loan amortizations payable to DBP;
The LBP account for GF shall be the only depository account of all collection receipts under such fund and shall be used for all GF disbursements for proper control and monitoring; and
Close the DBP account of MEEDO as authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) through an SB Resolution by transferring the remaining balance thereof in the LBP MEEDO account so that only one account for such fund shall be maintained pursuant to Section 3 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Treasurer trace up all purchases of accountable forms and corresponding issuances thereof per Invoice and Receipts and Report of Collections and Deposits records from prior years to present as basis for preparation of CRAAF. If warranted, a personnel be assigned to specifically do the same in order to establish ending balances of accountable forms and reconcile any differences with the actual balances and consequently set in place control over the receipts and issuance of accountable forms;
Henceforth, ensure that individual RAAF from each collecting/accountable officers are prepared and submitted; and
The Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the monthly CRAAF, duly supported with the individual RAAFs, to the Office of the Auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The BAC secure copies of Mayor’s/Business Permit and PhilGEPS Registration Number in its procurement thru small value procurement prior recommendation to HOPE to award the contract; and
The Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements are properly supported with complete documentation by requiring the submission of Mayor’s/Business Permit and PhilGEPS Registration Number of participating suppliers in case the municipality resorted to Small Value Procurement as its mode of procurement.

We recommended and the Management agreed to create a GAD database containing gender statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data which shall be regularly updated as basis in GAD Planning, programming and policy making.

We recommended and the Management agreed to modify/reconstitute its existing GFPS to conform with the structure and composition required under Section 4.1(2) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Immediately remit the remaining withheld taxes of prior months and make representation with the Bureau of Internal Revenue for the refund of the over-remittance of ₱12,219.23 under GF-MEEDO, if warranted; and
Ensure henceforth, that all the taxes withheld are remitted intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accounting Office expedite the tracing up and reconciliation of the over remittances.  Should it be established that there is indeed over remittance, payment from the concerned/liable personnel should be enforced;
The Municipal Accounting and Treasurer’s Offices ensure henceforth, that all withholdings for same are remitted intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges; and
Trace up the details of the “Due to GSIS” account balance of ₱240.00 under Special Education Fund and make the necessary adjustments, as appropriate.

Stop the withholding of business taxes for those establishments with no principal, branch, or sales outlet in the Municipality and to levy and collect business taxes in accordance with the Local Government Code and the Municipality’s Revenue Code.

The BAC and BAC Secretariat ensure that the observers required under Section 13.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 are invited and strictly observe the posting requirement of the Notice of Awards, Notice to Proceed, and Approved Contracts to the PhilGEPS and LGU’s website, when available, for its widest dissemination and for the full transparency of the agency’s procurement activities; and

The Municipal Engineer immediately submit the lacking documents as enumerated in Appendix H to facilitate the conduct of Technical Inspection on the completed projects of the municipality. Moreover, ensure that that pictures attached in every progress billing of the contractor are the true representation of the work accomplishments reported thereon.

The MA trace up and reconcile the unremitted GSIS contributions and follow-up the GSIS, in coordination with the Human Resource Officer, the updating of its records to effect the latest salaries of the municipal employees as well as that of the newly hired personnel so that unremitted balance pertaining thereto can be remitted to GSIS.

The MA and Budget Officer include in the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of DRRMF Sourced from GAA the 5% DRRM funds allocated from the municipality’s regular budget and all financial assistance from NDRRMF covered by SAROs, and ensure its timely submission on the fifth day following the end of each month to the concerned offices.

The MA consistently observe the perpetual inventory system in the recording/maintaining of inventory accounts and corresponding issuance thereof and observe the proper accounting of transactions based on its nature and classification; and

The MSWDO and the person in-charge of the monitoring and distribution of the relief goods properly identify the RDSs submitted in accordance to which wave it pertains. Trace up and prepare/submit a list of the composition of the food packs (name and quantity of items included in the food pack) distributed/released from the 1st to 4th wave of relief operation, duly signed by the accountable officer, to the Office of the Auditor for consideration.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the contractor to pay the unaccomplished work and impose the necessary liquidated damages from the delay/non completion of the project in accordance with the program of works without prejudice to other courses of action and remedies available under the circumstances.

The Municipal Engineer recommend to the Municipal Development Council the procurement of dump truck to avoid additional rental payments; and
If approved/considered and funds availability warrants, the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning Development Coordinator include in the Annual/Supplemental Investment Program the procurement of dump truck so that the same will be considered in preparing the Annual/Supplemental Budget taking into consideration the current cost of the equipment, and require the former to immediately undertake its procurement procedures.
We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for succeeding years of transfer of the Municipality’s unutilized DRRMF to the Special Trust Fund.

The LDRRMO and Municipal Accountant include in the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF all financial assistance from NDRRMF covered by SAROs, and ensure its timely submission on or before 15th day after the end of each month; and
The Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer include in the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of DRRMF Sourced from GAA the 5% DRRM funds allocated from the municipality’s regular budget and all financial assistance from NDRRMF covered by SAROs, and ensure its timely submission on the fifth day following the end of each month to the concerned offices.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:

The Accounting Office religiously maintain the PPELC to provide a detailed information of the PPE that will aide in the reconciliation against the Property Cards maintained by the Supply Officer/Property Custodian;

An Inventory and Appraisal Committee be created/updated to determine the appraised value of donated properties and those unserviceable properties found on station during the inventory taking so that these properties can be correctly recorded/ adjusted in the books of accounts of the municipality; and 

The General Services Office and the Accounting Office conduct reconciliation of their PPE records to ascertain their differences and to make the necessary adjustments in order to establish the completeness, accuracy and existence of the PPE accounts presented in the Financial Statements.

Ensure that the RPCPPE shall be reconciled with the accounting records so that the PPE account balances are fairly presented in the financial statements. The discrepancies should be investigated and necessary adjustments should be made to come up with a complete and accurate balance of PPE accounts in the financial statements

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:

The Municipal Accountant expedite the reconciliation of the unidentified fund transfers and maintain individual subsidiary ledgers for all fund transfers composing the controlling accounts “Due to NGAs” and “Due to LGUs”;

The Municipal Accountant identify/trace the negative balance of ₱6,253.50 in the Due to GOCCs account; and
The Municipal Accountant trace up the Memorandum of Agreement covering the fund transfers with completed PPAs but with fund balances as indicated in Appendix B and refund the remaining balance to the source agency, if required.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:

The Municipal Accountant consistently observe the perpetual inventory system in the recording/maintaining of inventory accounts and corresponding issuance thereof and observe the proper accounting of transactions based on its nature and classification;

The Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustment or correcting entry on the double posting of expense amounting to P108,500.00 thru debit to “Accounts Payable” account and credit to “Prior Year’s Adjustment”;

The Supply Officer prepare the SSMI and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant weekly for proper accounting of issuance of inventories, and conduct year-end physical count of inventory items to determine the undistributed items, if any, and the cost of these items shall be restored back to the inventory account; and

The MSWDO and the person in-charge of the monitoring and distribution of the relief goods observe and comply with the following:

Properly identify the RDSs submitted in accordance to which wave it pertains. Trace up and prepare/submit a list of the composition of the food packs (name and quantity of items included in the food pack) distributed/released from the 1st to 4th wave of relief operation, duly signed by the accountable officer, to the Office of the Auditor for consideration; and

Henceforth, ensure that all RDSs were fully filled out and duly signed to provide sufficient and reliable information and promote transparency especially on the items and quantity of the relief goods distributed to the beneficiaries and maintain a registry per article/item of goods to monitor/track its issuance and balances and to prepare and maintain a separate logbook to record the receipt and distribution of relief goods.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the duly signed BRS on a monthly basis to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary corrections/adjusting entries for any discrepancies/errors/other reconciling items in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made for all bank accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

The Municipal Treasurer to regularly update the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s Index Card which shall serve as the basis in the preparation of the List of Delinquent Real Property Tax Payers as of the end of the year, the total of which shall equal the RPT/SET Receivable at year-end; and to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible, and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant to serve as the basis in setting up the beginning balance of RPT/SET Receivable in the books of accounts. In such manner, the related receivable account and deferred liability will be presented fairly in the financial statements;

The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to conduct reconciliation of records based on the index card balances so that necessary adjustments could be made to bring the affected receivable and deferred accounts to its correct balance; and

The Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for RPT/SET Receivables based on the certified list of taxpayers and its corresponding amount due and collectibles from the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended the following:

Trace up the sales and receipts of the inventory items sold prior to the closure of the store and necessary adjustments should be made to properly take up the same;

The remaining unaccounted/non-existing costs of inventories may be debited to Prior Year’s Adjustments but should be disclosed in the notes to financial statements subject for proper accounting; and

Afterwards, disposal of the salable/usable items and/or condemnation of the damaged/expired items should be made so as to make the store space available for rent instead of being idle/stagnant.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the contractor to pay the unaccomplished work and impose the necessary liquidated damages from the delay/non completion of the project in accordance with the program of works without prejudice to other courses of action and remedies available under the circumstances. Likewise, please take note of other deficiencies noted, and make the necessary action to address or prevent further maintenance cost by the municipality.

We recommended the following:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the supporting schedules of the reported balances of the agency’s Receivable Account; and

Henceforth, ensure that the reported receivable account of the agency is existent and valid by making sure that the required subsidiary ledgers of the controlling accounts of the agency are timely prepared and/or updated coupled with complete supporting documents.

We recommended to the Accountant exhaust all means for the reconciliation of the Due to BIR account.  Should it be established that there is overpayment, cause the eventual refund from the individual involved and BIR and effect the proper adjustment in the books of accounts.

We recommended to the Accountant exhaust all means for the reconciliation of the Due to GSIS account.  Should it be established that there is overpayment, cause the eventual refund from the individual involved and GSIS and effect the proper adjustment in the books of accounts.

We recommended the following:

Prioritize the implementation of the recommendations of DENR-EMB Region VI on
the immediate rehabilitation and closure of the Municipal Disposal Site;

Materials Recovery Facility be established in every barangay or cluster of barangays.  The municipality may initially pilot a single barangay to establish MRF project in order to institute a system of knowledge in establishing one as well as to gain commitment/participation of barangay officials on the proper administration and the operation of MRF.

To ensure that regulations on the granting and liquidation of cash advance are strictly followed, we recommended to demand from the AO with outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2017 the immediate liquidation of such cash advances with the provision that if not liquidated within 30 days upon receipt of the demand letter, the same shall be deducted from their salaries.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the supporting schedules of the reported balances of the agency’s Receivable Account; and

Henceforth, ensure that the reported receivable account of the agency is existent and valid by making sure that the required subsidiary ledgers of the controlling accounts of the agency are timely prepared and/or updated coupled with complete supporting documents.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary corrections/adjusting entries for any discrepancies/errors/other reconciling items in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements. Also, bank reconciliation statements must be made of all bank accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended the following:
Conduct and make a report of the actual inventory of the remaining items in the grocery store including damaged/expired items and account for the value of the items which are still usable/salable and write-down its corresponding costs to its net realizable value.
Trace up the sales and receipts of the inventory items sold prior to the closure of the store and necessary adjustments should be made to properly take up the same.
The remaining unaccounted/non-existing costs of inventories may be debited to Prior Year’s Adjustments but should be disclosed in the notes to financial statements subject for proper accounting.
Afterwards, disposal of the salable/usable items and/or condemnation of the damaged/expired items should be made so as to make the store space available for rent instead of being idle/stagnant.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Ensure that the RPCPPE shall be reconciled with the accounting records so that the PPE account balances are fairly presented in the financial statements. The discrepancies amounting to ₱44.557 million should be investigated and necessary adjustments should be made to come up with a complete and accurate balance of PPE accounts in the financial statements;
Direct the Accounting Office to religiously maintain the PPELC to provide a detailed information of the PPE that will aide in the reconciliation against the Property Cards maintained by the Supply Officer/Property Custodian and henceforth, reconciliation of related subsidiary records should be regularly undertaken and regarded with utmost importance; and
A Municipal Valuation Committee be created to determine the appraised value of donated properties and those properties found on station during the inventory taking so that these properties can be correctly recorded in the books of accounts of the municipality.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the Municipal Accountant to compute the correct depreciation expenses of its assets and a journal entry must be drawn to update/correct the accumulated depreciation by a debit to depreciation expense (for the current year depreciation), debit to prior year’s adjustment (for the prior year’s depreciation) and a credit to accumulated depreciation (for the total unrecorded depreciation).
Exert effort through the General Services Office/Office of the Municipal Treasurer, to reconstruct and update its property records and reconcile with the accounting records to facilitate the setting up and periodic provision of depreciation for those depreciable assets not provided with depreciation.
Create an appraisal committee to conduct evaluation of assets whose records could not be found in order to establish the commencement of depreciation of the subjected assets and determine their depreciable cost and estimated remaining life for the computation of their respective depreciation.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that all depreciable properties owned by the LGU are provided with depreciation in accordance with the above-mentioned rules and regulations to ensure its fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Ensure that the required reports are prepared by the proper officials concerned, in accordance with the prescribed format, and submitted within the reglementary period; and
Remind the concerned officials to be mindful of their corresponding roles in the administration of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund in order to enhance the agency’s capacity to strengthen its disaster risk reduction and management system, through decentralized powers, responsibilities and resources.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the contractor to pay the unaccomplished work and impose the necessary liquidated damages from the delay/non completion of the project in accordance with the program of works without prejudice to other courses of action and remedies available under the circumstances. Likewise, please take note of other deficiencies noted, and make the necessary action to address or prevent further maintenance cost by the municipality.

We recommended that hereafter, the Municipality to faithfully observe the 60-day period within which to submit to the audit team from receipt of the succeeding AARs, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the strict compliance of the Municipal Accountant in the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements considering the materiality of the fund balance in order to validate and ascertain the correctness of reconciling items and effect the necessary adjustments and corrections in the accounting records. If warranted, assign a qualified additional staff in the Office of the Municipal Accountant to assist her in the reconciliation.
 
Moreover, the Municipal Accountant is advised to make representations with the depository banks for complete copies of bank statements and bank debit/credit advices within the timeframe set forth in the abovementioned regulation.

a)	The Accountant exhaust all means for the reconciliation of the Due to BIR account.  Should it be established that there is overpayment, cause the eventual refund from the individual involved and BIR and effect the proper adjustment in the books of accounts; and

b)	The Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the taxes withheld intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.

a)	The Accountant exhaust all means for the reconciliation of the Due to GSIS account.  Should it be established that there is overpayment, cause the eventual refund from the individual involved and GSIS and effect the proper adjustment in the books of accounts; and

b)	The Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the contributions and loan amortizations withheld intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.

The Municipal Council for the Protection of Children be reorganized to update its mandated duties and responsibilities in conformity with Rule 8 of Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9344, as amended, to fully uphold the essence of the institutionalization of LCPC and to clearly sets out the duties and responsibilities of the council in strengthening the juvenile justice and welfare of children at risk and children in conflict with the law from prevention to rehabilitation and reintegration; and

The management to direct the members of the MCPC to prepare the LDPC, LIPC, LSCR and Children’s Profile in compliance with the Section II.1 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Local Social Welfare and Development Office and Local Planning and Development Office to develop their 3-year Comprehensive Local Juvenile Intervention Program in accordance with the guidelines for the LGU on the Development of the CLJIP, electronic copy of which can be downloaded from the JJWC National Secretariat website, pursuant to DILG MC No. 2016-68.

We recommended that the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain the PC, PPELC and RPLC, respectively, in accordance with Section 119 and 120 of MNGAS for NGAs, Volume I, following the prescribed formats under Annexes 9, 11 and 37 of the same manual, Volume II.

Thereafter, a reconciliation of balances should be made between the accounting records and RPCPPE. Verify any noted discrepancy and effect the necessary adjustments.

To ensure that regulations on the granting and liquidation of cash advance are strictly followed, we recommended the following:

Demand from the AO with outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2017 the immediate liquidation of such cash advances with the provision that if not liquidated within 30 days upon receipt of the demand letter, the same shall be deducted from their salaries; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger for each AO with cash advances complete with details: purpose, date granted, Check No., date of check, JEV No. date of liquidation etc. to ensure proper monitoring of the liquidation on cash advances granted as required by existing regulation.

To ensure that regulations on the granting and liquidation of cash advance are strictly followed, we recommended the following:

Demand from the AO with outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2017 the immediate liquidation of such cash advances with the provision that if not liquidated within 30 days upon receipt of the demand letter, the same shall be deducted from their salaries;

Require the Municipal Accountant to certify on the face of the voucher for cash advances that the AO has no previous outstanding or unliquidated cash advances; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger for each AO with cash advances complete with details: purpose, date granted, Check No., date of check, JEV No. date of liquidation etc. to ensure proper monitoring of the liquidation on cash advances granted as required by existing regulation.
observations.reason: (There are still outstanding cash advances that remained unliquidated)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the strict compliance of the Municipal Accountant in the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements considering the materiality of the fund balance in order to validate and ascertain the correctness of reconciling items and effect the necessary adjustments and corrections in the accounting records. If warranted, assign a qualified additional staff in the Office of the Municipal Accountant to assist her in the reconciliation.

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant is advised to make representations with the depository banks for complete copies of bank statements and bank debit/credit advices within the timeframe set forth in the abovementioned regulation.

We recommended the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the supporting schedules of the reported balances of the agency’s Receivable Account; and
Henceforth, ensure that the reported receivable account of the agency is existent and valid by making sure that the required subsidiary ledgers of the controlling accounts of the agency are timely prepared and/or updated coupled with complete supporting documents.

We recommended the following:
a) 	The Accountant exhaust all means for the reconciliation of the Due to BIR account.  Should it be established that there is overpayment, cause the eventual refund from the individual involved and BIR and effect the proper adjustment in the books of accounts; and
b) 	The Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the taxes withheld intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.

We recommended the following:
a) 	The Accountant exhaust all means for the reconciliation of the Due to GSIS account.  Should it be established that there is overpayment, cause the eventual refund from the individual involved and GSIS and effect the proper adjustment in the books of accounts; and
b) 	The Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the contributions and loan amortizations withheld intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Council for the Protection of Children be reorganized to update its mandated duties and responsibilities in conformity with Rule 8 of Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9344, as amended, to fully uphold the essence of the institutionalization of LCPC and to clearly sets out the duties and responsibilities of the council in strengthening the juvenile justice and welfare of children at risk and children in conflict with the law from prevention to rehabilitation and reintegration; and
The management to direct the members of the MCPC to prepare the LDPC, LIPC, LSCR and Children’s Profile in compliance with the Section II.1 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Local Social Welfare and Development Office and Local Planning and Development Office to develop their 3-year Comprehensive Local Juvenile Intervention Program in accordance with the guidelines for the LGU on the Development of the CLJIP, electronic copy of which can be downloaded from the JJWC National Secretariat website, pursuant to DILG MC No. 2016-68.

We recommended that the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain the PC, PPELC and RPLC, respectively, in accordance with Section 119 and 120 of MNGAS for NGAs, Volume I, following the prescribed formats under Annexes 9, 11 and 37 of the same manual, Volume II.

Thereafter, a reconciliation of balances should be made between the accounting records and RPCPPE. Verify any noted discrepancy and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: (Reconciliation of PPE is still on going)

observations.recommendation:
To ensure that the same deficiency be avoided, we recommended to the management the following:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact the daily collections with the authorized depository bank (AGDB), within a day or within a week if collection reaches ₱5,000.00 or more considering the distance of the AGDB from the municipality.  To ensure that this is being done, require her to submit for notation by your good office all copies of validated deposits slips daily or regularly;

Require the accountable officer to submit monthly report of collections and deposits duly certified by the Municipal Accountant to be posted in the bulletin of the Municipality of New Washington as a regular monitoring tool; and

After three (3) written reminders to the accountable officer, file an appropriate administrative case against the said accountable officer.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
(The LCE has issued series of memos to the AO mandating the regular deposit, but Instances of undeposited collections were still noted)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to update the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements in order to identify and ascertain the validity of reconciling items and effect the necessary adjustments and corrections in the accounting records. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant is advised to make representations with the depository banks for complete copies of bank statements and bank debit and credit advices within the timeframe set forth in the above mentioned regulation.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented
(Bank Reconciliation Statement was still not regularly prepared)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to renew the LTO registration to ensure issuance of government plates. Likewise, secure copies of the Official Receipts/Certificates of Registration (OR/CR) as proof of legitimate ownership of the Municipality of New Washington, apply for the comprehensive vehicle insurance.

We recommended that the Department Head of the Municipal Agriculture and Aquatic Resources facilitate the immediate use of the Fish Landing Center and the amenities included thereon for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.  Likewise, secured the area from people whose activities are not related neither to fish/aquatic matters nor for the benefit of the fisher folks.
observations.reason:
(According to the LCE, she has mandated the use of the facility and assigned personnel therein, however, according to the OIC Municipal Agriculturist, the Fish Landing Center is not fit for public use and operation due to the following:

It is not fit for public use and operation of Fish Landing due to questionable structural soundness of existing seawall which will be use as primary landing area.

The sub base course of the seawall has been wash out by the strong sea current; and

The location or site used for landing area is not ideal for the above mentioned purpose since the sea level in said area at high tide reaches the upper portion of the seawall posing danger and damages to small boats or vessels during strong waves or currents.)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain the PC, PPELC and RPLC, respectively, in accordance with Section 119 and 120 of MNGAS for NGAs, Volume I, following the prescribed formats under Annexes 9, 11 and 37 of the same manual, Volume II, format attached as Annexes B-D.

Thereafter, a reconciliation of balances should be made between the accounting records and RPCPPE. Verify any noted discrepancy and effect the necessary adjustments.

The management should also ensure the timely submission of the required reports so as to give ample time for its review and verification.

To ensure that this is being done, require her to submit for notation by your good office all copies of validated deposits slips daily or regularly;
Require the accountable officer to submit monthly report of collections and deposits duly certified by the Municipal Accountant to be posted in the bulletin of the Municipality of New Washington as a regular monitoring tool; and
After three (3) written reminders to the accountable officer, file an appropriate administrative case against the said accountable officer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to update the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements in order to identify and ascertain the validity of reconciling items and effect the necessary adjustments and corrections in the accounting records. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant is advised to make representations with the depository banks for complete copies of bank statements and bank debit and credit advices within the timeframe set forth in the abovementioned regulation.

To ensure that regulations on the granting and liquidation of cash advance are strictly followed, we recommended the following:

Demand from the AO with outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2017 the immediate liquidation of such cash advances with the provision that if not liquidated within 30 days upon receipt of the demand letter, the same shall be deducted from their salaries;
Require the Municipal Accountant to certify on the face of the voucher for cash advances that the AO has no previous outstanding or unliquidated cash advances; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger for each AO with cash advances complete with details: purpose, date granted, Check No., date of check, JEV No. date of liquidation etc. to ensure proper monitoring of the liquidation on cash advances granted as required by existing regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly follow the rules and regulations, particularly on the timeline in the submission of year end trial balances and financial statements prescribed under Section 41 (2) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the LCE requires the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of PPE respectively to facilitate reconciliation of records.
observations.reason:
(PCs and PPELCs were prepared, however, not per category as required by Sec. 119 and 120 of MNGAS for LGUs, Vol. I, hence
reiterated as Finding no. 1)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

a. The Assistant Municipal Treasurer deposit intact the daily collections with the authorized depository bank, within a day or within a week if collection reaches P5,000.00 or more considering the distance of the AGDB from the municipality;

b.Stop the practice of retaining part of the collections as cash reserve for cash disbursements; and

c.Require the Municipal Treasurer to regularly monitor remittance and deposit of collections of the collecting officers and the Assistant Municipal Treasurer.

To ensure that regulations on the granting and liquidation of cash advance are strictly followed, we recommended the following:

Demand from the AO with outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2016 the immediate liquidation of such cash advances with the provision that if not liquidated within 30 days upon receipt of the demand letter, the same shall be deducted from their salaries;

Require the Municipal Accountant to certify on the face of the voucher for cash advances that the AO has no previous outstanding or unliquidated cash advances; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger for each AO with cash advances complete with details: purpose, date granted, Check No., date of check, JEV No. date of liquidation etc. to ensure proper monitoring of the liquidation on cash advances granted as required by existing regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to update the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements in order to identify and ascertain the validity of reconciling items and effect the necessary adjustments and corrections in the accounting records. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant is advised to make representations with the depository banks for complete copies of bank statements and bank debit and credit advices within the timeframe set forth in the abovementioned regulation.
observations.reason:
(Incomplete records of bank statements and the corresponding debit and credit advices)
Reiterated as Finding no. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to renew the LTO registration to ensure issuance of government plates. Likewise, secure copies of the Official Receipts/Certificates of Registration (OR/CR) as proof of legitimate ownership of the Municipality of New Washington, apply for the comprehensive vehicle insurance.
observations.reason: (2 out of 4 units were registered to the Municipality)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the putting up of signage and installation of electric lights for the safety of the motorists and passersby.  Likewise, the Municipal Engineer should always consider all aspects in project planning and implementation including public safety as the motto goes “Safety First”.  Projects can be implemented phase by phase hence, preparation of plans and program of works should consider the whole project/outcome to avoid wastage of time, money and effort.

We recommended that the Department Head of the Municipal Agriculture and Aquatic Resources facilitate the immediate use of the Fish Landing Center and the amenities included thereon for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.  Likewise, secured the area from people whose activities are not related neither to fish/aquatic matters nor for the benefit of the fisher folks.
observations.reason:
(According to the OIC Municipal Agriculturist, the Fish Landing Center is not fit for public use and operation due to the following:

It is not fit for public use and operation of Fish Landing due to questionable structural soundness of existing seawall which will be use as primary landing area, reconstruction and rehabilitation of the said portion of the seawall must first be undertaken to insure public safety;

The sub base course of the seawall has been wash out by the strong sea current; and

The location or site used for landing area is not ideal for the above mentioned purpose since the sea level in said area at high tide reaches the upper portion of the seawall posing danger and damages to small boats or vessels during strong waves or currents.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Officer in Charge of the Office of the Municipal Agriculture identify the causes why the said tractor cannot be used.  If found unfit for use, then, recommend the return to the DA-RFO VI as stipulated in the MOA.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant enforce daily the submission of all the reports and documents from the Municipal Treasurer. Then all submitted reports and supporting documents by the accountable officers shall immediately be recorded in the books of accounts of which the same shall be submitted within ten (10) days of the ensuing month  to the  Office of the Audit Team Leader as mandated under Section 7.2 and 7.3 of COA Cir. No. 94-006 dated February 17, 1994.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly follow the rules and regulations in submission of year end trial balances and financial statements as prescribed under Section 41 (2) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly follow the rules and regulations, particularly on the timeline in the submission of year end trial balances and financial statements prescribed under Section 41 (2) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the LCE requires the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of PPE respectively to facilitate reconciliation of records.

The Assistant Municipal Treasurer deposit intact the daily collections with the authorized depository bank, within a day or within a week if collection reaches P5,000.00 or more considering the distance of the AGDB from the municipality.
Stop the practice of retaining part of the collections as cash reserve for cash disbursements; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to regularly monitor remittance and deposit of collections of the collecting officers and the Assistant Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to update the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements in order to identify and ascertain the validity of reconciling items and effect the necessary adjustments and corrections in the accounting records.  Moreover, the Municipal Accountant is advised to make representations with the depository banks for complete copies of bank statements and bank debit and credit advices within the timeframe set forth in the abovementioned regulation.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to renew the LTO registration to ensure issuance of government plates. Likewise, secure copies of the Official Receipts/Certificates of Registration (OR/CR) as proof of legitimate ownership of the Municipality of New Washington, apply for the comprehensive vehicle insurance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the putting up of signages and installation of electric lights for the safety of the motorists and passersby.  Likewise, the Municipal Engineer should always consider all aspects in project planning and implementation including public safety as the motto goes “Safety First”.  Projects can be implemented phase by phase hence, preparation of plans and program of works should consider the whole project/outcome to avoid wastage of time, money and effort.

We recommended that the Department Head of the Municipal Agriculture and Aquatic Resources facilitate the immediate use of the Fish Landing Center and the amenities included thereon for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.  Likewise, secured the area from people whose activities are not related to fish/aquatic matters nor for the benefit of the fisher folks.

We recommended that the Officer in Charge of the Office of the Municipal Agriculture identify the causes why the said tractor cannot be used.  If found unfit for use, then, recommend the return to the DA-RFO VI as stipulated in the MOA.

We recommend that a committee shall be created to conduct physical count of PPE in CY 2015, prepare a RPCPPE and require the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of PPE respectively to facilitate reconciliation of records.
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Assistant Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all their collections with the AGDB, daily or not later than the next banking day.

We recommend that Management submit the legal basis for the exemptions, if any so that the excess expenditure in PS Cost may be passed in audit.  Also, submit justification why such were incurred despite the declaration of the SP of the approved budget to be “inoperative in part”.

We recommend that Management require collectors to turnover their collections to the Municipal Treasurer intact daily and regularly.  The Municipal Treasurer should also require the Barangay Treasurer to remit their collections on a weekly or bi-monthly basis

We recommend that management require the liquidation of cash advances within the time required by law. Further, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant should monitor the liquidation of cash advances by sending demand letters regularly and  the Municipal Treasurer should withhold payment of any money due them in case of their failure to  liquidate their cash  advances.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to update the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements in order to identify and ascertain the validity of reconciling items and effect the necessary adjustments and corrections in the accounting records. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant is advised to make representations with the depository banks for complete copies of bank statements and bank debit and credit advices within the timeframe set forth in the abovementioned regulation.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant:

a.Prepares and submits a certificate of availability of funds in every expenditures/obligations charged against the Trust Fund, which shall be attached to and become an integral part of the same; and 

b.Prepare a CAF pro-forma ready in order that the same should not be overlooked and ensure that all future transactions against TF be supported by the said certificate.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:

a.Require the MGSO and BAC Chairman to submit to the office of the auditor, copy of the contracts and POs together with the supporting documents within five (5) working days upon perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.  
b.Require the BAC Secretariat to properly accomplish the PO, to include all necessary information to determine the legality and propriety of the transactions.
c.Instruct the Municipal Officials concerned to strictly follow the provision of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and the format of the PO prescribed in Annex 29, Vol. 2 of MNGAS for LGUs, copy hereto attached for reference.
